_comment: Policy for the Catalog Writeback (Phase CW) feature — silent component extraction during user deploy/publish, admin-gated promotion to mchatai-source main. The Mac app reads triggerSignalWeights + extractor restrictions at capture time. The web admin Cloud Functions (runComponentDiscriminator, promoteComponentCandidate) read the rest at promotion time. Mirrors template-promotion-policy.json but routes promoted artifacts to frameworks/<platform>/components/ instead of harness/templates/. See planning doc ah-i-just-had-declarative-glade.md.
_minConfidenceComment: Tighter than the template review threshold (0.50) because catalog corruption is asymmetric in cost — a bad component poisons every future generation that hits its keywords, whereas a bad template just wastes a slot in the catalog list. Web admin can opt-in to lower-confidence candidates via a filter.
_triggerWeightsComment: Lower than template equivalents (1.5 / 2.0) because component candidates have weaker provenance — the Lego is one extracted slice of a deployed app, not the app itself. Public publish still weighs higher than private deploy because the user committed to sharing the parent app.
_targetBaseComment: Promoted components land at frameworks/<platform>/components/<category>/<slug>.<ext> with a sibling CONTEXT.md in <slug>/. Platform = web-components / macos-components / mobile-components / pipelines.
_subdirComment: Per-platform parallel directory structure under frameworks/. Renaming a subdir is a content change here + a git mv — no Swift code change required.
_extractorProviderComment: HARD allow-list. The component-extraction LLM call is restricted to Claude Code or OpenAI Codex only — lower-tier models produce malformed _index.json patches, broken markers, or weak keywords that pollute the catalog. Adding any other provider here MUST be matched in CW.3's ProposerKind.componentExtraction routing.
_discriminatorProviderComment: The DISCRIMINATOR (run by Cloud Function on the candidate after it lands) can be a smaller model — it's just classifying promote/decline + suggesting slug/category. The EXTRACTOR (run by Mac app to produce the candidate) is restricted by extractorProviderAllowList. Different gates.
_discriminatorModelComment: Same models the template discriminator uses. The component discriminator prompt lives at agents/prompts/component-discriminator.md.
_autoFilterComment: If a category already has N components in the live _index.json, any new candidate in it must beat 0.80 discriminator confidence to surface in the default review queue. Prevents catalog bloat in saturated categories. Empty map = no auto-filter.
_screeningCapComment: When admin opens the queue or a screening Cloud Function fires, screen at most this many unscreened candidates per call. Bound API spend on an aged backlog.
_qualityGateComment: Strict gate (per user direction 2026-05-04): only sessions with rubric ≥13/14, ≤2 fix attempts, build clean, and EITHER thumbs-up OR ≥7 days with no negative feedback are eligible for extraction. Cluster threshold (minSameKeywordSessions) requires ≥2 sessions hit the same matcher-miss cluster before extraction fires — one anecdote isn't enough.
_validatorComment: All 7 rules must pass before a candidate is staged. Failure logs to component-extraction-attempts.jsonl and the candidate is silently dropped. End user is never notified of validator outcomes.
